title: veronica alpina subsp. lasiocarpa
intro: Veronica alpina subsp. lasiocarpa, also known as Alpine Speedwell, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Veronica alpina subsp. lasiocarpa include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues, including colds, fever, and digestive problems.
